feature_flag_model.provider: Server-side feature flag service (evaluated per-tenant, per-user)

evaluation_strategy.caching: Client SDK caches flags for 5 minutes, refresh on app resume
evaluation_strategy.fallback_on_error: Use cached flags; if no cache, all W1 features default ON, W2+ default OFF

dependency_graph.description: Maps each wave's components to their required backend services, socket namespaces, and Kafka topics

blocking_dependencies: Schema: post, comment, reaction schemas in realtime-contracts

compliance-service.notes: Report handling can use existing compliance pipeline

progression_rules: Waves MUST be rolled out sequentially: W1 → W2 → W3 → W4
A wave can only begin rollout when ALL blocking dependencies are resolved
Initial rollout to internal tenants (dogfooding) for minimum 2 weeks
Percentage rollout: 1% → 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% with 48h hold at each stage
Rollback trigger: error rate > 1% or p99 latency > 2x baseline

progressive_enablement.strategy: Feature flags enable individual components within a wave
progressive_enablement.example: W1 can roll out ChatWindow and ChatList first, then SearchBar and GroupManager in a follow-up
progressive_enablement.constraint: Components with hard dependencies (e.g., MessageInput requires ChatWindow) must be enabled together

event_schema_mismatch.detection: Consumer receives event with unknown fields or missing required fields → schema validation error
response: 1. Log validation error to audit-events topic with full event payload
2. If error rate > 0.1% of events in 5 minutes → trigger feature flag kill switch for affected wave
3. Kill switch disables all components in the affected wave for all users
4. Preserve events in dead-letter queue for replay after schema fix
5. Alert on-call team via PagerDuty with schema mismatch details

component_failure.detection: Client-side error boundary catches render/runtime error
response: 1. Error boundary renders fallback UI with 'Something went wrong' message
2. Error reported to observability pipeline with component name, props snapshot, stack trace
3. If same component error from > 100 unique users in 10 minutes → auto-disable feature flag
4. User sees graceful degradation (e.g., ChatWindow error → falls back to ChatList only)

backend_unavailability.detection: SDK circuit breaker opens for a required backend service
response: 1. Components dependent on unavailable service show fallback UI (as defined in component catalog)
2. If service remains unavailable > 15 minutes → disable wave feature flags for new connections
3. Existing connected users retain cached state but cannot perform write operations

dashboards: Wave Rollout Health: Per-wave error rate, latency, adoption
Feature Flag Status: Flag evaluation counts, override distribution
Schema Compatibility: Validation error rate by topic and version
